name: paper-mamba-synthesis
description: Use web_search to search for 'Mamba SSM future outlook transformer replacement subquadratic attention'. Results overlap with earlier SSM searches. Synthesize: will SSMs replace transformers? Summarize the consensus view, key strengths, and remaining gaps. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: Strengths: Mamba provides linear-time training and constant-memory inference, achieving 5x throughput over Transformers while matching their quality at equivalent scale, with proven scaling laws and production adoption in models like Jamba and Granite 4.0. Weaknesses: Pure SSMs still lag on in-context learning, associative recall, and multi-hop reasoning tasks due to their finite recurrent state bottleneck and optimization instability; practical deployments predominantly use hybrid SSM-attention architectures. Outlook: The 2026 consensus favors hybrids (1-in-8 attention ratio) as the default architecture, with pure SSMs dominating long-context and edge scenarios; the SSD framework's proof that SSMs and attention are mathematically dual suggests future convergence toward modular architectures that dynamically switch between paradigms based on task demands.
key_facts: Mamba achieves 5x throughput over Transformers with linear-time complexity and constant inference memory
Pure SSMs still lag on in-context learning and retrieval, making hybrids the production default
The 2026 consensus favors hybrid architectures with approximately 1-in-8 attention-to-SSM layer ratio
SSD framework proved mathematical duality between SSMs and attention via semiseparable matrices
Production adoption includes IBM Granite 4.0, AI21 Jamba, and Mistral Codestral Mamba
